Get early access

Onboarding in waves. Work emails get priority.

No spam. GDPR-compliant. Privacy Policy applies.

Academic researchers are invisible to B2B tools.

LinkedIn profiles are wrong or empty. ZoomInfo misses them entirely. Academic databases are 3 years stale. If your buyer is a PI, lab manager, or industry-adjacent scientist — you have no reliable way to reach them.

🚫

LinkedIn is guesswork

70% of researchers have no LinkedIn presence. Those who do rarely update institutional details or grant affiliations.

📅

ZoomInfo misses academia

Commercial databases index corporate HR systems. University researchers and NIH-funded PIs simply don't appear.

🕳️

Databases are 3 years stale

Faculty directories and publication databases update yearly at best. Lab moves, funding changes, and industry pivots go unrecorded.

How it works

Three steps from search intent to verified contact export.

1

Search by field, topic, or funding

Query 250M+ papers. Filter by research area, active NIH/NSF grants, institutional affiliation, or publication recency. Find exactly the scientists you need.

# Example query
field:"CRISPR gene editing"
funding:"NIH R01"
institution:"Stanford"
2

Filter by industry signals

Surface researchers with one foot in industry: patent holders, startup co-founders, SAB members, industry-funded PIs, and recent academia→industry transitions.

patent_holder: true startup_affiliation: true industry_funding: true
3

Export verified contact + intent data

Get institutional email addresses, ORCID profile, H-index, recent publications, grant amounts, and conference appearances — all in one export.

CSV export CRM sync REST API

Who uses ResearchReach

Built for teams that sell to, recruit from, or invest in the scientific community.

🧬

Pharma & Biotech Outreach

Find PIs with active drug discovery grants. Identify researchers who've co-authored with industry teams. Build KOL lists with verified contacts, not stale rosters.

Learn more →
🖥️

Scientific Software Sales

For Benchling, Dotmatics, and ELN vendors. Reach lab managers and PIs who are actively publishing in your target verticals. Segment by lab size and funding level.

Learn more →
🔬

Deep Tech Recruiting

Source PhD researchers with industry bridge signals. Filter by patent filings, startup co-founder credits, or recent transition from academia to industry.

Learn more →
💰

VC & Deal Sourcing

Track researchers filing patents in emerging fields before they spin out. Monitor NIH funding in your thesis areas. Surface the scientists most likely to found deep-tech companies.

Learn more →

We aggregate the world's best open research infrastructure — then layer proprietary industry signal detection on top.

Frequently asked questions

How fresh is the data? +

We sync with OpenAlex and Semantic Scholar weekly, PubMed daily, and NIH Reporter monthly. ORCID profiles are pulled on-demand. Most records are updated within 7 days of a new publication or funding award.

Is this GDPR-compliant? +

Yes. We only index publicly-disclosed contact information (institutional emails listed in publications or on university pages) and public grant records. Researchers can request removal via our opt-out form. We follow GDPR legitimate interest grounds for B2B contact data.

How accurate are the email addresses? +

We target >85% deliverability on verified contacts. Institutional emails are cross-referenced against recent publications (last 24 months) to filter out stale records. We mark addresses as "high confidence", "likely valid", or "unverified" so you can prioritize.

What's the pricing model? +

We're finalizing pricing. Early access users will lock in launch pricing. We plan a credit-based model (pay per export) with monthly subscription tiers for heavy users. API access will be separate. Join the waitlist to be first to know.

Is there an API for programmatic access? +

Yes. We're building a REST API for scientific software vendors and data teams. See the Developers page for the schema and early access program. Python and Node.js SDK planned at launch.

How is this different from regular research databases like Scopus or Web of Science? +

Scopus and WoS are citation databases — built for academic research, not B2B prospecting. They don't surface contact information, industry signals, or grant data in a usable way. ResearchReach is a contact intelligence tool first: we're optimized for outreach and deal sourcing, not literature review.

Ready to find the right scientists?

Join the waitlist. We're onboarding teams in waves — priority to use cases with the tightest fit.
